info.name: Bifrost Routing Wiring (Governance x Catalog)
info.description: Governance × model-catalog routing wiring. Each scenario stands up an isolated, run-namespaced custom provider backed by real OpenAI, gates it with keys and a virtual key, then drives inference and asserts the route via extra_fields.routing_info (success), the error message (rejection), and the stored log. Machine-generated by runners/build-routing-wiring.mjs — do not hand-edit.

exec: pm.test('setup: list providers', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.equal(200); });
pm.test('setup: delete provider', function () { pm.expect([200, 204, 404], 'status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()).to.include(pm.response.code); });

item.name: VK allows model, key allows — request routes
item.description: A VK whose allowed_models includes the request, over a key that allows it, routes successfully and records the key.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('route status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()); }

item.name: VK pinned to a disabled key is unroutable even with an enabled sibling
item.description: The VK restricts to k1, which is disabled; k2 is enabled but VK-excluded, so key selection finds no usable key and rejects with 400.

item.name: Both keys serve under weighted key selection
item.description: Two enabled, equal-weight keys on one provider (no VK). Over a batch of requests, core's weighted key selection routes through both keys.

exec: mustServe.forEach(function (e) { if (seen.indexOf(e) < 0) throw new Error('key ' + e + ' never served; observed ' + JSON.stringify(seen)); });

item.name: A weightless VK is an allow-list (no LB), confirmed by the routing log trail
item.description: Two providers on a VK with NO weights: A's key serves gpt-4o-mini, B's serves only gpt-4o. Routing the bare gpt-4o-mini, governance filters by capability (excludes B) and — having no weighted configs — skips load balancing, routing to A. The routing_engine_logs record the allow-list decisions.

item.name: Routing the resolved model directly (not the alias) is rejected
item.description: A key gates the alias name, not the resolved id. Routing the alias resolves and succeeds; routing the resolved model id directly fails the gate — alias targets are not auto-added to the key's Models.

item.name: 03. alias routes (resolves to the model id) [reverse-alias-gate]

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('route status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()); }

if (!ri.resolved_key_alias || ri.resolved_key_alias.model_id !== "gpt-4o-mini") { throw new Error('resolved_key_alias=' + JSON.stringify(ri.resolved_key_alias)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }

item.name: An alias defined on two keys resolves to the last key
item.description: Two keys define the same alias to different models. The alias resolves to the last-defined key's target and is served by that key.

item.name: A request resolves to an alias whose name differs only in case

item.name: A request fails over to a healthy provider via a request-level fallback
item.description: The primary provider's key is invalid, so its attempt fails upstream; a request-level fallback to a second provider serving the same model succeeds. routing_info marks the fallback and records the original primary provider/model. The /v1 endpoint takes fallbacks in the string "provider/model" form.

if (Boolean(ri.is_fallback) !== true) { throw new Error('is_fallback=' + ri.is_fallback); }
if (ri.primary_provider !== expectedPrimary) { throw new Error('primary_provider=' + ri.primary_provider + ' expected ' + expectedPrimary); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }

item.description: Under a VK that permits only the primary provider, a request-level fallback to a healthy off-allowlist provider is pruned before the attempt loop. The primary's invalid key fails with a 401 and the pruned provider — which has a valid key and would otherwise return 200 — never rescues it, proving it was dropped.

item.description: A VK weights two providers that both serve the model; the dominant-weight provider's key is invalid. item.name: VK with no provider configs blocks everything
item.description: An empty provider_configs is deny-by-default; the provider is not permitted.
